message.content:
Adversaries may use custom methods to compress or encrypt data before exfiltration, bypassing standard detection mechanisms. Techniques include XOR, stream ciphers, and custom implementations of well-known algorithms like Lempel–Ziv–Welch (LZW), Blowfish, and AES. Examples include ADVSTORESHELL using LZW, Attor with Blowfish and RSA, and Machete with AES. These methods obscure data, making it harder to detect and analyze.

The impact of this technique is significant as it complicates data loss prevention and forensic analysis. Adversaries can evade detection tools that rely on recognizing standard compression or encryption signatures.

Common exploitation methods involve custom encryption schemes, such as XOR with dynamic keys, substitution ciphers, and custom compression algorithms. Notable groups like FIN6, Lazarus Group, and Mustang Panda have employed these techniques.

Mitigation strategies include monitoring for unusual data compression or encryption activities, implementing robust data loss prevention (DLP) solutions, and employing advanced threat detection systems capable of identifying non-standard encryption and compression methods. Regularly updating threat intelligence and employing behavioral analysis can also help in detecting and mitigating such sophisticated techniques.
